::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## Phase B vs. Phase C: how syndication is parameterised

Phase B's §3d specifies the external cession as a single ratio
$\beta \in [0, 1]$ — the share of the $(1 - \alpha)$ stochastic leg
sold to a third-party counterparty at $t = 0$. The retained P&L is
linear in $I_T$ and every closed-form moment carries over from §3a–§3c.

Phase C applies $\beta$ to the **residual stochastic leg of the custom
book** rather than to an idealised $(1 - \alpha)$ slice: the Phase C
coverage is $\tau = \min(1, k / (N \cdot P))$, not $\alpha$, so the
deterministic shift is $(Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}})$ and the
residual carries everything else — the uncovered-tail integral
$J_\tau$ and, when the inventory is over-sized, the terminal
mark-to-spot $k_{\mathrm{left}} \cdot S_T$. Ceding $\beta$ of that
residual gives

$$
\Pi_{\mathrm{ret}}
  = (Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}})
  + (1 - \beta) \cdot \bigl( \Pi_{\mathrm{cust}} - (Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}}) \bigr)
  + \pi.
$$

| Phase B | Phase C equivalent |
| --- | --- |
| $\beta$ | $\beta$ (same symbol) |
| Ceded mass $\beta \cdot (1-\alpha) \cdot \Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}}$ | Ceded mass $\beta \cdot (\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}} - (Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}}))$ |
| Premium $\pi = \beta(1-\alpha)\bigl(\mathbb{E}[\Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}}] - \rho(\theta)\bigr)$ | Premium $\pi = \beta \bigl(\mathbb{E}[\mathrm{residual}] - \rho(\theta) \cdot \mathrm{SD}[\mathrm{residual}]\bigr)$ |
| Closed-form premium (Dufresne moments) | MC-moment-derived premium (custom inventory has no clean closed form) |

The MC-moment premium is the deliberate Phase C choice: with the
custom-inventory specification there is no closed-form shortcut for
$\mathbb{E}[\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}}]$ in the over-hedged regime, so the
premium is priced off the same sample tape the histograms are drawn
from. The counterparty's pricing model is therefore *consistent with
whatever the slider produces* — a useful stress-test lens.
:::
